description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, jagged surface and a predominantly pinkish-brown coloration. The rock displays a natural, unpolished texture with visible mineral speckling and subtle striations running across its surface. One side of the rock features a small, dark marking or inscription, possibly indicating identification or cataloging. The edges are uneven and angular, with some faces appearing flatter and others more sharply contoured. The rock's mass and density suggest it is composed primarily of granite or a similar hard stone. Its overall appearance is consistent with rocks used as natural barriers or decorative elements in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A rough, pinkish-brown granite rock with jagged edges, mineral speckling, and a visible marking on one side. Its irregular shape and natural texture make it suitable as an urban barrier or landscaping feature.
description_view_0: The main face of the rock is visible, showing a broad, flat surface with a dark marking or inscription and a mottled pink-brown coloration.
description_view_1: A narrow, angular edge is seen, highlighting the rock's thickness and the rough, jagged nature of its profile.
description_view_2: The top-down view reveals the full extent of the rock's irregular outline and speckled mineral patterning.
description_view_3: A side view displays a more vertical, blocky face with visible texture and color variation, emphasizing the rock's mass.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: There is no visible evidence of intentional integration with urban infrastructure.
